task: Fix CodeMie CLI SSO authentication on Windows: always print the SSO URL as a manual fallback and use explorer.exe instead of open() to avoid WDAC/AppLocker-blocked PowerShell dependency

key_reasoning.reason: CodeMieSSO.authenticate() is entirely untested; the fix introduces a novel explorer.exe spawn path (no codebase precedent); the URL print must precede open() because WDAC silently swallows the open() call without throwing an exception; an inline comment is required to justify divergence from the deliberate PowerShell choice in mcp-oauth-provider.ts:162-168 — all well-understood risks but requiring care in implementation and test authoring

1–2 source files modified (sso.auth.ts primary, browser.ts optionally) plus test additions to the existing sso.auth.test.ts; all within the sso plugin directory and optionally utils; no new files needed; the bulk of effort is writing new test scenarios (platform override, spawn mock, URL print assertion, macOS regression)

red_flags_applied: Technical Risk bumped from S (2) to M (3): change is inside the SSO authentication setup flow, triggering the auth/authorization red flag
